titleStmt.title: SII 1.35: original edition by Eugen Hultzsch
PART I. SANSKRIT INSCRIPTIONS. II. COPPER-PLATE GRANTS OF THE EASTERN CHALUKYA DYNASTY. No. 35. A GRANT OF NARENDRA-MŠĻöIGARńÄJA.

summary.p: The subjoined five grants belong to the kings Narendra-mŠĻõigarńĀja or VijayńĀditya II, Amma I. or VishŠĻáuvardhana VI, ChńĀlukya-Bhńęma II. or VishŠĻáuvardhana VII, Amma II. or VijayńĀditya V. and Vńęra-ChoŠłća or VishŠĻáuvardhana IX. The place, which is occupied by each of these princes in the genealogy of the Eastern Chalukya dynasty, will be seen from the annexed table, for which all hitherto published Eastern Chalukya grants have been consulted, and in which numbers are prefixed to the names of those princes who really reigned, in order to mark their succession. For previous lists of the Eastern Chalukya dynasty, see Sir Walter Elliot's Numismatic Gleanings, No. 2, in the Madras Journal, New Series, Vol. IV, pp. 81 f. and the same scholar's Coins of Southern India, p. 87; Dr. Burnell's South-Indian Palaeography, pp. 21 f. and Sewell's Lists of Antiquities, Vol. II, p. 152.

The relation of the two usurpers (18) TńĀlapa and (21) Yuddhamalla to the direct line of the family is established by three inscriptions:‚ÄĒa. TńĀŠłćapa is called the son of VikramńĀditya's brother (Ind. Ant. Vol. XIV. p. 56); b. TńĀla is called the son of Yuddhamalla, who was the paternal uncle of ChńĀlukya-Bhńęma I. (Ind. Ant. Vol. XIII, p. 249, where pitŠĻõivya has to be read for pitŠĻõivyo); c. Bhńęma II, the son of KollabhigaŠĻáŠłća VijayńĀditya, is at the same time called the son of Yuddhamalla, the son of TńĀlapa, i.e., he belonged to the next generation after (21) Yuddhamalla (Ind. Ant. Vol. XII, p. 92).
Three of the last kings, who are shown in the annexed table, viz., (28) VijayńĀditya VI, (29) RńĀjarńĀja II. and (30) Vńęra-ChoŠłća, are only known from the subjoined inscription No. 39.

This grant belongs to the Sir W. Elliot Collection of the British Museum, and was made over to me for publication by Dr. Burgess. It consists of five copper-plates with raised rims. Each plate measures 9 by 3 inches. The first and fifth plates are inscribed only on their inner sides, while the three middle ones bear writing on both sides. The preservation of the plates is tolerably good. They are strung on an elliptic ring, which is (1/2)" thick and 4(7/8)" by 3(1/2)" in diameter. The well-preserved circular seal, which is attached to the ring, measures 2(5/8)" in diameter. It bears the sun and the moon at the top, the legend ŇõrńętribhuvanńĀŠĻĀkuŇõa across the centre, and an expanded lotus-flower (side-view) at the bottom‚ÄĒall in relief on a counter-sunk surface.

The document is a grant of the parama-mńĀheŇõvara Narendra-mŠĻõigarńĀja, alias VijayńĀditya II., the son of VishŠĻáuvardhana IV. and grandson of VijayńĀditya I. The name of the district (vishaya), to the inhabitants of which the king addresses his order, is lost. On the occasion of a lunar eclipse (chandra-grahaŠĻáa-nimitteCompare uttarńĀyaŠĻáanimitte in No. 37, and YńĀj√Īavalkya, I, 203: dńĀtavyaŠĻĀ pratyahaŠĻĀ pńĀtre nimitteŠĻ£u viŇõeŠĻ£ataŠł•, where the MitńĀksharńĀ explains nimitteŠĻ£u by candroparńĀgńĀdiŠĻ£u. The nimittas are specified in an inscription from Dholpur, which I have published in the Zeitschr. d. Deutsch. Morg. Gesellschaft, Vol. XL, p. 38, where king ChaŠĻáŠłćamahńĀsena is said to have made gifts rńĀhŇęparodhaparvaŠĻái, saŠĻĀkrńĀntau and ayanńĀdńĀŠĻĀ.) the king gave the village of KoŠĻüŠĻüapaŠĻüŠĻüu to twenty-four brńĀhmaŠĻáas. Of these, six adhered to the HiraŠĻáyakeŇõi-sŇętra and eighteen to the ńÄpastamba-sŇętra. They belonged to the following gotŠĻõas:‚ÄĒAgniveŇõya, KauŠĻáŠłćinya, KauŇõika, Gautama, ParńĀŇõara, BhńĀradvńĀja, Vatsa, ŇöńĀŠĻáŠłćilya, SaŠĻĀkŠĻõiti and Harita. According to the colophon of the grant, ‚Äúthe excellent prince NŠĻõipa-Rudra, who was the brother of Narendra-mŠĻõigarńĀja and a descendant of the Haihaya-vaŠĻĀŇõa (!), (was) the executor of this charity.‚ÄĚńÄj√Īaptir asya dharmasya; ńĀj√Īapti, which occurs in several other grants, is evidently a synonym of dŇętaka ‚Äú(royal) messenger;‚ÄĚ see Mr. Fleet's note 37, Ind. Ant. Vol. VIII, p. 79. In other inscriptions we find ńĀj√ĪńĀ (Vol. XIII, p. 122, note 45) and ńĀj√ĪńĀdńĀpaka (Vol. XIV, p. 161, note 28).

[[genealogical table:]] PEDIGREE OF THE EASTERN CHALUKYA DYNASTY. [C1]Kńęrtivarman (until Ňöaka 489). [C1]SatyńĀŇõraya Vallabha (from Ňöaka 532 until at least 556).See Mr. Fleet's Kanarese Dynasties, page 23. [C2]1. Kubja VishŠĻáuvardhana I. VishamasiddhiThe surname Vishamasiddhi is found on the seals of some grants and in a grant published in Dr. Burnell's South-Indian Palaeography, 2nd edition, page 137, plate xxvii, line 4. See also Ind. Ant. Vol. VII, p. 185. (18 years; cir. Ňöaka 526-27 to cir. 544-45). [C1]2. JayasiŠĻĀha I. Vallabha (33 years; cir. Ňöaka 544-45 to cir. 577-78).30 years according to Ind. Ant. Vol. VII, page 16; Vol. XIV, page 56. [C2]3. Indra BhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠ńĀraka.According to the three latest inscriptions (Ind. Ant. XIV, 52, 56 and No. 39) he ruled 7 days. [C1]4. VishŠĻáuvardhana II. (9 years; cir. Ňöaka 577-78 to cir. 586-87.) [C1]5. MaŠĻÖgi-yuvarńĀja (25 years; cir. Ňöaka 586-87 to cir. 611-12). [C1]6. JayasiŠĻĀha II. (13 years; cir. Ňöaka 611-12 to cir. 624-25.) [C2]7. Kokkili (6 months; cir. Ňöaka 625). [C3]8. VishŠĻáuvardhana III. (37 years; cir. Ňöaka 625 to cir. 662.) [C1]9. VijayńĀditya I. BhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠ńĀraka (18 years; cir. Ňöaka 662 to cir. 680). [C1]10. VishŠĻáuvardhana IV. (36 years; cir. Ňöaka 680 to cir. 716.) [C1]11. VijayńĀditya II. Narendra-mŠĻõigarńĀja (48 years; cir. Ňöaka 716 to cir. 764).40 years according to No. 37 and Ind. Ant. VIII, 77; 44 years according to No. 36. [C1]12. Kali VishŠĻáuvardhana V. (1(1/2) years; cir. Ňöaka 764 to cir. 765-66.) [C1]13. GuŠĻáaga, GuŠĻáagńĀŠĻÖka or GuŠĻáakenalla VijayńĀditya III. (44 years; cir. Ňöaka 765-66 to cir. 809-10.) [C2]YuvarńĀja VikramńĀditya. [C3]Yuddhamalla. [C1]14. ChńĀlukya-Bhńęma I. DrohńĀrjuna (30 years; cir. Ňöaka 809-10 to cir. 839-40.) [C1]15. KollabhigaŠĻáŠłća, KollabigaŠĻáŠłća or KaliyarttyaŠĻÖka VijayńĀditya IV. (6 months; cir. Ňöaka 840.) [C1]16. Amma I. VishŠĻáuvardhana VI. RńĀjamahendra (7 years; cir. Ňöaka 840 to cir. 847). [C1]17. VijayńĀditya.He reigned (1/2) month according to No. 37 and Ind. Ant. XIII, 249. [C1]18. TńĀha, TńĀla, TńĀŠłćapa, TńĀlapa or TńĀŠłĽapa (1 month; cir. Ňöaka 847). [C1]19. VikramńĀditya (11 months or 1 year; cir. Ňöaka 847 to cir. 848). [C2]20. Bhńęma.[[missing]] [C1]21. Yuddhamalla (7 years; cir. Ňöaka 848 to cir. 855).He is left out altogether in No. 37 and Ind. Ant. XIII, 249. [C1]22. ChńĀlukya-Bhńęma II. VishŠĻáuvardhana VII. GaŠĻáŠłćamahendra, son of queen MeŠłĽńĀmbńĀ (12 years; cir. Ňöaka 855 to 867). [C1]23. Amma II. VijayńĀditya V. son of queen LokamahńĀdevńę, ascended the throne in Ňöaka 867[[missing]] and reigned 25 years (to cir. Ňöaka 892). [C1]24. DńĀnńĀrŠĻáava or DńĀna-nŠĻõipa (3 years; cir. Ňöaka 892 to cir. 895). [C1]25. Aftor an interregnum of 27 years,[[missing]] Ňöaktivarman or ChńĀlukyachandra reigned 12 years; circa Ňöaka 925 to circa 937. [C2]26. VimalńĀditya married KŇęndavńĀ, daughter of RńĀjarńĀja of the SŇęrya-vaŠĻĀŇõa and younger sister of RńĀjendra-ChoŠłća (7 years; cir. Ňöaka 937 to 944). [C1]27. RńĀjarńĀja I. VishŠĻáuvardhana VIII. married AmmaŠĻÖga-devńę, daughter of RńĀjendra-ChoŠłća of the SŇęrya-vaŠĻĀŇõa, ascended the throne in Ňöaka 944[[missing]] and reigned 41 years (to Ňöaka 985). [C2]28. VijayńĀditya VI. received the kingdom of VeŠĻÖgńę from his nephew RńĀjendra-ChoŠłća and reigned 15 years (Ňöaka 985 to 1000). [C1]RńĀjendra-ChoŠłća, alias KulottuŠĻÖga-ChoŠłća-deva I, KulottuŠĻÖga-deva or RńĀjanńĀrńĀyaŠĻáa, ChoŠłća king, married MadhurńĀntakńę, daughter of RńĀjendradeva of the SŇęrya-vaŠĻĀŇõa, and reigned 49 years (Ňöaka 985 to 1034). [C1]Vikrama-ChoŠłća (15 years; Ňöaka 1034 to 1049). [C2]29. RńĀjarńĀja II. (1 year; Ňöaka 1000 to 1001.) [C3]30. Vńęra-ChoŠłća VishŠĻáuvardhana IX. ascended the throne in Ňöaka 1001; a grant[[missing]] is dated in the 21st year of his reign (Ňöaka 1022). [C4]Four other sons. [C1]KulottuŠĻÖga-ChoŠłća-deva II. was reigning in Ňöaka 1056.[[missing]]

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . ŇõrńęmatńĀŠĻĀ sakalabhuvanasaŠĻĀstŇęyamńĀnamńĀnavyasagotrńĀŠĻáńĀŠĻĀ hńĀritńęputrńĀŠĻáńĀŠĻĀ
kauŇõikńęvaraprasńĀdalabdharńĀjyńĀnńĀŠĻĀ mńĀtŠĻõgaŠĻáaparipńĀlitńĀnńĀŠĻĀ svńĀmimahńĀsenapńĀ
dńĀnudhyńĀtńĀnńĀŠĻĀ bhagavannńĀrńĀyaŠĻáaprasńĀdasamńĀsńĀditavaravarńĀhalńĀ√ĪchanekŠĻ£aŠĻáa
kŠĻ£aŠĻáavaŇõńękŠĻõtńĀrńĀtimaŠĻáŠłćalńĀnńĀ AmaŇõvamedhńĀvabhŠĻõtthasnńĀnapavitrńękŠĻõtavapu
ŠĻ£ńĀŠĻĀ caŠł∑ukyńĀnńĀŠĻĀ kulamalaŠĻĀkaripŠĻáoŠł•ranekasamaramaŠĻĀghaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠alabdhanijabhujavija
yaŇõrńęiyaŠł• niravadyodńĀraguŠĻáagaŠĻáńĀlaŠĻĀkŠĻõtasya ŇõrńęvijayńĀdityamahńĀrńĀjasya
pautraŠł• sphuritakaravńĀŠł∑adhńĀrńĀvaŇõńękŠĻõtńĀrńĀtibhŇęmaŠĻáŠłćalasya svacaritanyakkŠĻõta
nŠĻõganaŠł∑anahuŠĻ£ńĀŠĻĀvarńęŠĻ£ayayńĀter viŠĻ£ŠĻáoriva svacakranandakasya viŠĻ£ŠĻáuvarddhanamahńĀ
rńĀjasya priyatanayaŠł• pratńĀpńĀnurńĀgńĀvanatasamastasńĀmantamaulńęlńĀŠł∑itaŇõńĀsanaŠł•
kŠĻ£atrocitaŇõaktitrayapńĀtrńębhŇętaŠł• AnekasaŠĻĀgrńĀmavijayńĀsńĀditavikramadhavaŠł∑aŠł• ya
madaŠĻáŠłćacaŠĻáŠłćadorddaŠĻáŠłćamaŠĻáŠłćalńĀgranakhakhaŠĻáŠłćitaripukarigaŠĻáŠłćasthaŠł∑eo narendramŠĻõga
rńĀjńĀaŠł• ArńĀtiŠĻ£aŠłćvargganigrahakaraŠł• samadhigatarńĀjńĀavidyńĀcatuŠĻ£ŠĻ≠ayaŠł• caturupńĀyapra
yogacaturaŠł• duŠĻ£ŠĻ≠ńĀanigrahaŇõiŠĻ£ŠĻ≠ńĀnugrahakaraŠł• madhumathana Iva svavikramńĀkrńĀntabhuvanaŠł•
yudhiŠĻ£ŠĻ≠hira Iva bhńęmńĀrjunaparńĀkramasahńĀyaŠł• daŇõarathasuta Iva sńętńĀnandanakaraŠł• manuri
va sanmńĀrggadarŇõŇõńę padmńĀkńĀara Iva AritimiranikaravidhvaŠĻĀsanńĀdityaŠł• paramabrahmaŠĻáyaŠł• paramamńĀhe
ŇõvaraŠł• samastabhŇęuvanńĀŇõrayaŇõrńęvijayńĀdityamahńĀrńĀjńĀdhirńĀjaparameŇõvarabhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠ńĀrakaŠł•
vńĀdiviŠĻ£ayanivńĀsino rńĀŠĻ£ŠĻ≠rakŇęŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠apramukhaŠł•kŇęuŠĻ≠uŠĻĀmbinaŠł• sarvvńĀnitthamńĀj√ĪńĀpayati . viditamastu
vaŠł• AbhipendaŠĻáŠłćuruvńĀstavyńĀya kauŇõikagotrńĀya hiraŠĻáyńĀakeŇõisŇętrńĀya veda
vedńĀŠĻĀgapańĀragńĀya veŠĻáamaŇõarmmaŠĻáe nalŇęcerivńĀstavyńĀya kauŇõikagotrńĀya hi
raŠĻáyakeŇõisŇętrńĀya caŠĻ≠iŇõarmmaŠĻáe podeŠĻĀguvńĀstavyńĀya gautamagotrńĀya hiraŠĻáya
keŇõisŇętrńĀya vidaŇõarmmaŠĻáe podaŠĻĀguvńĀstavyńĀya saŠĻĀkrńęŠĻõti gotrńĀya hiraŠĻáyńĀakeŇõisŇę
trńĀya maviŠĻáŠłćiŇõarmmaŠĻáe podeŠĻĀguvńĀstavyańĀya AharitagotrńĀya hiraŠĻáyńĀakeŇõisŇętrńĀya yńĀ
j√ĪaŇõarmmaŠĻáe podeŠĻĀguvańĀstavyńĀya saŠĻĀkriŠĻõtigotrńĀya hiraŠĻáyńĀakeŇõisŇętrńĀya Ňõarmma
ŠĻáe krovńĀŇõirivańĀstavyńĀya AharitagotrńĀya ApastambasŇętrńĀya kńĀmaŇõarmma
ŠĻáe UrpuŠĻ≠ŇęruvańĀstavyńĀya bhńĀradvńĀjagotrńĀya ApastambasŇętrńĀya
viŠĻ£ŠĻáuŇõarmmaŠĻáe vaŠĻĀgipaŠĻüŠĻüuvańĀstavyńĀya kauŠĻáŠłćinyagotrńĀya ApastambambasŇętrńĀ
ya gu√ĪjadevaŇõarmmaŠĻáe vaŠĻĀgipaŠĻüŠĻüuvańĀstavyńĀya ŇõńĀŠĻáŠłćilyagotrńĀya Apa
stambasŇętrńĀya bhadraŇõarmmaŠĻáe vaŠĻĀgipaŠĻüŠĻüuvańĀstavyńĀya kauŠĻáŠłćinyagotrńĀya Apa
stambasŇętrńĀya viŠĻ£ŠĻáuŇõarmmaŠĻáe vaŠĻĀgipaŠĻüŠĻüuvańĀstavyńĀya kauŠĻáŠłćinyagotrńĀya A
pastambasŇętrńĀya nńĀrńĀyaŠĻáaŇõarmmaŠĻáe cńĀnturuvańĀstavyńĀya bhńĀradvńĀjagotrńĀ
ya ApastambasŇętrńĀya droŠĻáaŇõarmmaŠĻáe cńĀnturuvańĀstavyńĀya bhńĀradvńĀ
jagotrńĀya ńÄpastambasŇętrńĀya nńĀrńĀyaŠĻáaŇõarmmaŠĻáe krovaŇõirivańĀstavyńĀ
ya haritagotrńĀya ńÄpastambasŇętrńĀya mńĀdhavaŇõarmmaŠĻáe krovaŇõirivańĀsta
vyańĀya parasńĀŇõaragotrańĀya ApastambasŇętrńĀya vennamaŇõarmmaŠĻáe krovaŇõirivańĀstavyńĀya vatsago
trńĀya ApastambasŇętrńĀya ArudiŇõarmmaŠĻáe UrpuŠĻ≠ŇęruvańĀstavyńĀya bhńĀradvńĀjagotrńĀya
ApastambasŇętrńĀya nandiŇõarmmaŠĻáe kńĀrahaiduvańĀstavyńĀya bhńĀradvńĀjagotrńĀya Apa
stambasŇętrńĀya viŠĻ£ŠĻáuŇõarmmaŠĻáe kńĀrahaiduvańĀstavyńĀya bhńĀradvńĀjagotrńĀya ApastambasŇętrńĀ
ya bhńĀramńĀŇõarmmaŠĻáe kńĀrahaiduvańĀstavyńĀya bhńĀradvńĀjagotrńĀya ApastambasŇętrńĀya ya√ĪjaŇõa
rmmaŠĻáe cńĀnturuvańĀstavyńĀya bhńĀradvńĀjagotrńĀya ApastambasŇętrńĀya bńĀdadiŇõarmmaŠĻáe krńĀja
vańĀstavyńĀya kauŠĻáŠłćinyagotrńĀya ApastambasŇętrńĀya veŠĻáŠĻáaŇõarmmaŠĻáe rńĀyŇęruvańĀstavyńĀ
ya AgnńęiveŇõyagotrńĀya ApastambasŇętrńĀya ŠĻüompayaŇõarmmaŠĻáe vedavedńĀŠĻĀgaratebhyaŠł•
ŠĻ£aŠĻ≠karmmaniratebhyaŇõ catruurviŠĻĀŇõńĀtibrńĀhmaŠĻáebhyńĀaŇõ candruagrahaŠĻáanimitte UdakapŇę
rvvavaŠĻĀ koŠĻüŠĻüapaŠĻüŠĻüu nńĀma grńĀmassarvvakaraparihńĀraŠĻĀ kŠĻõtvńĀ dattaŠł• . AsyńĀvadhivicańĀraŠł• pŇęrvvataŠł• A
tŇęgupaŠĻüŠĻüu dakŠĻ£iŠĻáataŠł• vńĀnapaŠĻüŠĻüu paŇõcimataŠł• vńĀŠĻáŠłćhaŠĻüŇępedayŇę UttarataŠł• ganayyńĀra
bu caturviŇõatyai dattaŠł• . Asyopari na kenacidbńĀdhńĀ karaŠĻáńęyńĀ . karoti yassa pa
√ĪcamahńĀpńĀtakasaŠĻĀyukto bhavati . vyńĀsenńĀpyuktaŠĻĀ . bahubhirvvasudhńĀ dattńĀ bahubhiŇõcńĀnu
pńĀlitńĀ . yasya yasya yadńĀ bhŇęmistasya tasya tadńĀ phalaŠĻĀ . svadńĀattńĀŠĻĀ paradattńĀŠĻĀ vńĀ yo hareta
vasundharńĀŠĻĀ . ŠĻ£aŠĻ£ŠĻ≠ivaruŠĻ£asahasrńĀŠĻái viŠĻ£ŠĻ≠hńĀyńĀŠĻĀ jńĀyate kŠĻõmiŠł• . kalpakoŠĻ≠isahasrńĀŠĻái
svarge modati bhŇęmidaŠł• . AńÄkŠĻ£eptńĀ cńĀnumantńĀ ca tńĀnyeva narake vaset¬∑ . rńĀmeŠĻáńĀpyu
ktaŠĻĀ . sarvvńĀnevaŠĻĀ bhńĀvinaŠł• pńĀrtthivendrńĀn¬∑ bhŇęyo bhŇęyo yńĀcate rńĀmabhadraŠł• . sńĀmńĀnyoyaŠĻĀ dharmmase
turnŠĻõpńĀŠĻáńĀŠĻĀ kńĀle kńĀle pńĀlanńęyo bhavadbhiŠł• . narendramŠĻõgarńĀjasya bhrńĀtańĀ haihayavaŠĻĀŇõajaŠł• . ńÄj√Īapti
rasya dharmmasya rnŠĻõparudranŠĻõipottamaŠł• . vijayavńĀdavńĀstavyńĀyaena AkŠĻ£aralalitńĀcńĀryyeŠĻáa likhitaŠĻĀ .

Hail ! The grandson of the illustrious VijayńĀditya-mahńĀrńĀja, who was adorned with a multitude of blameless and noble virtues, who had acquired the splendour of victory by his own arm in many warlike encounters, and who adorned the race of the glorious ChaŠł∑ukyas, who belong to the gotra of the MńĀnavyas, who are praised in the whole world; who are the sons of HńĀritńę; who have acquired the kingdom by the favour of (Ňöiva) the husband of KauŇõikńę; who are protected by the assemblage of (divine) mothers; who are meditating at the feet of the lord MahńĀsena (Skanda); who have subdued the territories of their enemies in an instant at the (mere) sight of the sign of the boar, a boon which they had obtained through the favour of the blessed NńĀrńĀyaŠĻáa (VishŠĻáu); and whose bodies are purified by bathing at the end of horse-sacrifices;‚ÄĒ

The beloved son of VishŠĻáuvardhana-mahńĀrńĀja, who subdued the surrounding territories of his enemies with the edge of his flashing sword, who surpassed by his deeds NŠĻõiga, Nala, Nahusha, Ambarńęsha and YayńĀti, and who rejoiced in his sovereignty, just as VishŠĻáu in his discus;‚ÄĒ

He whose commands are cherished by the diadems of all vassals who bow affectionately to (his) majesty, who is a receptacle of the three powersThe three Ňõaktis are prabhńĀva, utsńĀha and mantra. which are suitable to the warrior-caste, who is resplendent with the strength which he has acquired by victories in many battles, Narendra-mŠĻõigarńĀja, who has cut the temples of the elephants of his foes with the sword (that resembles) a clawThe king's sword is compared to a claw and consequently Narendra-mŠĻõigarńĀja (i.e., the lion among princes) himself to a lion or to NarasiŠĻĀha. in his arm which is as fierce as Yama's rod, who has subdued the six (internal) enemies,These are kńĀma, krodha, lobha, harsha, mńĀna and mada. who has acquired the four branches of royal science, who knows how to employ the four expedients,The four upńĀyas are sńĀman, dńĀna, bheda and deŠĻáŠłća. who chastises the wicked and shows favour to the good, who has conquered the world by his valour, just as (VishŠĻáu) the destroyer of Madhu by his (three) stops, who is assisted by terrible and splendid courage, just as YudhishŠĻ≠hira was assisted by the courage of Bhńęma and Arjuna, who, just as (RńĀma) the son of DaŇõaratha, gives pleasure to SńętńĀ,In the case of the king, this seems to mean, that he was a favourite of Lakshmńę, with whom SńętńĀ is identified. who knows the right course, just as Manu, who disperses the crowd of his foes, just as the sun disperses the mass of darkness in a lotus-group,As ‚Äúthe lotus-group‚ÄĚ does not suit the context, I suspect that something has fallen out after padmńĀkara iva. the most pious one, the devout worshipper of MaheŇõvara (Ňöiva), the asylum of the whole world, the illustrious VijayńĀditya, the king of great kings, the supreme master, the lord, thus issues his commands to all householders, (viz.) heads of provinces, etc., who inhabit the district of . . . . .

(Line 17.) ‚ÄúBe it known to you, (that I) gave on the occasion of a lunar eclipse, with a libation of water, the village called KoŠĻüŠĻüapaŠĻüŠĻüu, making (it) exempt from all taxes, to (the following) twenty-four brńĀhmaŠĻáas, who are engaged in (the study of) the Vedas and VedńĀŠĻÖgas and intent on (the performance of) the six duties, (viz.) to VeŠĻáama-Ňõarman, who dwells at AbhipendaŠĻáŠłćuru, belongs to the KauŇõika-gotra, follows the HiraŠĻáyakeŇõi-sŇętra and is well versed in the Vedas and VedńĀŠĻÖgas; to ChaŠĻ≠i-Ňõarman, who dwells at NalŇęcheri, belongs to the KauŇõika-gotra and follows the HiraŠĻáyakeŇõi-sŇętra; to Vida-Ňõarman, who dwells at PodeŠĻÖgu, belongs to the Gautama-gotra and follows the HiraŠĻáyakeŇõi-sŇętra; to MaviŠĻáŠłći-Ňõarman, who dwells at PodaŠĻÖgu,This place is elsewhere called PodeŠĻÖgu. belongs to the SaŠĻĀkŠĻõiti-gotra and follows the HiraŠĻáyakeŇõi-sŇętra; to YńĀj√Īa-Ňõarman, who dwells at PodeŠĻÖgu, belongs to the Harita-gotra and follows the HiraŠĻáyakeŇõi-sŇętra; to . . Ňõarman, who dwells at PodeŠĻÖgu, belongs to the SaŠĻĀkŠĻõiti-gotra and follows the HiraŠĻáyakeŇõi-sŇętra; to KńĀma-Ňõarman, who dwells at KrovńĀŇõiri,Elsewhere called KrovaŇõiri. belongs to the Harita-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to VishŠĻáu-Ňõarman, who dwells at UrpuŠĻ≠Ňęru, belongs to the BhńĀradvńĀja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to Gu√Ījadeva-Ňõarman, who dwells at VaŠĻÖgipaŠĻüŠĻüu, belongs to the KauŠĻáŠłćinya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to Bhadra-Ňõarman, who dwells at VaŠĻÖgipaŠĻüŠĻüu, belongs to the ŇöńĀŠĻáŠłćilya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to VishŠĻáu-Ňõarman, who dwells at VaŠĻÖgipaŠĻüŠĻüu , belongs to the KauŠĻáŠłćinya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to NńĀrńĀyaŠĻáa-Ňõarman, who dwells at VaŠĻÖgipaŠĻüŠĻüu, belongs to the KauŠĻáŠłćinya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to DroŠĻáa-Ňõarman, who dwells at ChńĀnturu, belongs to the BhńĀradvńĀja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to NńĀrńĀyaŠĻáa-Ňõarman, who dwells at ChńĀnturu, belongs to the BhńĀradvńĀja-gotra and follows the ńÄpastamba-sŇętra; to MńĀdhava-Ňõarman, who dwells at KrovaŇõiri, belongs to the Harita-gotra and follows the ńÄpastamba-sŇętra; to Vennama-Ňõarman, who dwells at KrovaŇõiri, belongs to the ParńĀŇõara-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to Arudi-Ňõarman, who dwells at KrovaŇõiri, belongs to the Vatsa-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to Nandi-Ňõarman, who dwells at UrpuŠĻ≠Ňęru, belongs to the BhńĀradvńĀja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to VishŠĻáu-Ňõarman, who dwells at KńĀrahaidu, belongs to the BhńĀradvńĀja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to BhńĀramńĀ-Ňõarman, who dwells at KńĀrahaidu, belongs to the BhńĀradvńĀja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to Ya√Īja-Ňõarman, who dwells at KńĀrahaidu, belongs to the BhńĀradvńĀja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to BńĀdadi-Ňõarman, who dwells at ChńĀnturu, belongs to the BhńĀradvńĀja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; to VeŠĻáŠĻáa-Ňõarman, who dwells at KrńĀja, belongs to the KauŠĻáŠłćinya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sŇętra; and to ŠĻěompaya-Ňõarman, who dwells at RńĀyŇęru, belongs to the AgniveŇõya-gotra and follows the ‚ÄúApastamba-sŇętra.‚ÄĚ

(Line 43.) (There follows) the description of the boundaries of this (village.)This is left out in the translation, because the reading of the proper names is very uncertain owing to the bad preservation of this part of the grant. . . . . . . . . .

(Line 45.) Nobody shall cause obstruction to this (grant); he, who does it, becomes possessed of the five great sins. VyńĀsa also has said: [Here follow three of the customary imprecatory verses, which it is unnecessary to translate.]
(Line 49.) RńĀma also has said: [Here follows another verse.]
(Line 51.) The executor (ńĀj√Īapti) of this charity (was) the excellent prince NŠĻõipa-Rudra, who was the brother of Narendra-mŠĻõigarńĀja and a descendant of the Haihaya race.
(Line 52.) (This edict) was written by AksharalalitńĀchńĀrya, who dwelt at VijayavńĀda.
Digital edition of SII 1.35 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
See also edition by Daniel Balogh (KoŠĻüŠĻüapaŠĻüŠĻüu grant of VijayńĀditya II ).
